commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a postpublish verifier denylist for deprecated `@mariozechner/pi-*` runtime imports in root `dist` files and a regression test for `@mariozechner/pi-ai/oauth`.

Reproducibility: yes. from source for the verifier gap: current main accepts declared runtime dependencies, and `@mariozechner/pi-ai/oauth` normalizes to the declared package name. I did not execute the verifier or inspect a real installed package artifact in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Maintainer/contributor action is needed for real behavior proof and the linked-issue closing reference; I found no narrow code defect for an automated repair PR.

target.title: [codex] Guard published dist against deprecated pi runtime imports
target.body:
Fixes #84095.

## Summary

- Add a postpublish verification guard that rejects root `dist` artifacts importing deprecated `@mariozechner/pi-*` runtime packages, even when those package names are still declared in `package.json`.
- Add a regression test covering the stale `@mariozechner/pi-ai/oauth` import pattern.
- Catch the packaging failure mode behind #84095 before a broken artifact can ship again.

## Why

Issue #84095 reported that Feishu DM sessions on OpenClaw 2026.5.12 never exposed `feishu_doc`, `feishu_drive`, and related tools, even though the Feishu plugin was enabled and channel tool config was correct.

The failure mode we traced here was stale published root `dist` output still importing deprecated `@mariozechner/pi-*` runtime packages such as `@mariozechner/pi-ai/oauth`. After rebuilding the artifacts so those imports pointed at `@earendil-works/pi-ai/oauth`, the effective Feishu DM tool inventory included the Feishu document tools again. This patch does not change Feishu runtime behavior directly; it hardens the release guard so that a package containing the stale runtime import pattern is rejected before publish.

## Verification

- `pnpm test test/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check scripts/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.ts test/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Behavior addressed: Published root `dist` artifacts must not retain deprecated `@mariozechner/pi-*` runtime imports that break plugin runtime wiring and hide Feishu tools in DM sessions.
Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout; issue root-cause investigation reproduced earlier in this thread against stale vs rebuilt root `dist` artifacts.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm test test/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.test.ts`, `pnpm exec oxfmt --check scripts/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.ts test/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.test.ts`, and `git diff --check`; earlier in the investigation, rebuilt the package artifacts and re-resolved the effective Feishu DM tool inventory.
Evidence after fix: Rebuilt root `dist` imports `@earendil-works/pi-ai/oauth` instead of `@mariozechner/pi-ai/oauth`, and the effective Feishu DM tool inventory included `feishu_doc`, `feishu_drive`, `feishu_wiki`, and related tools again.
Observed result after fix: The postpublish verifier now fails any packaged install whose root `dist` still imports the deprecated runtime package scope, preventing the broken artifact pattern from shipping.
What was not tested: No live Feishu DM session or published npm install was rerun in this turn; this patch adds the packaging guard and regression coverage.
target.author: nianjiuzst
